name: Division #6 Highway
description_short: Federal credit union serving members with checking, savings, mortgage loans, and consumer lending. NCUA-insured with membership-based structure.
description_long:
Division #6 Highway Credit Union is a federally insured credit union based in Town and Country, Missouri, serving members through a single physical location. The organization has a long operational history, evidenced by their 73rd Annual Membership Meeting referenced on their website. As a credit union, it operates on a membership model rather than traditional shareholder structure, with governance involving member participation in annual meetings.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of consumer and mortgage financial products. Their mortgage lending includes fixed-rate options at 6.00% (15-year), 6.25% (20-year), and 6.50% (30-year terms). They provide checking and savings accounts with free online bill pay, debit cards with 24/7 customer service, Visa credit cards, home equity lines of credit, vehicle loans (new and used), signature loans, and share-secured loans. Members can access e-statements and online banking services.

Division #6 Highway operates with limited physical accessibility, maintaining single office hours (Monday-Friday, 8am-4pm) and a single branch location. They recently relocated to 14323 S. Outer Forty Road, Suite 100 South in Chesterfield, Missouri. The credit union emphasizes membership communication and requests that members notify them of travel plans, suggesting a personalized, member-focused approach typical of smaller community credit unions.

The organization provides legitimate financial services with NCUA federal insurance backing deposits up to $250,000. However, the single branch and limited hours may present accessibility challenges for members who work standard business hours or require in-person services outside their schedule. Their mortgage rates (6.0%-6.5%) appear competitive at the time of website publication, though rates require verification with current market conditions.
services: Checking accounts with free online bill pay
Visa credit cards with customer service
Online banking and bill pay services
pros: Federal NCUA insurance on deposits up to $250,000 with full faith and credit backing
Free online bill pay included with checking accounts
24/7 debit card customer service (833-933-1681 US, 812-647-9794 international)
Mortgage lending available with fixed rates (6.0%-6.5% depending on term)
Multiple loan products including home equity LOC, vehicle loans, and signature loans
E-statement option available to reduce paper waste
Member-focused governance with annual membership meetings and community involvement
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for members outside Town and Country, Missouri area
Limited business hours (8am-4pm Monday-Friday) may not accommodate working members needing service
Website does not display current rates, requiring direct contact for accurate mortgage/loan pricing
No mention of online account opening or digital-first banking capabilities
Appears to be a very small institution with limited geographic reach compared to larger credit unions
best_for: Local Missouri residents with established credit seeking personalized credit union banking
Members prioritizing federal insurance protection and membership-based financial institutions
Consumers interested in fixed-rate mortgage loans and home equity products
Individuals seeking 24/7 debit card support with local in-person service access
diagnosis: Division #6 Highway Credit Union is best for individuals living in or near Town and Country, Missouri who value membership-based banking with federal insurance and personalized service. The primary caveat is severe geographic and temporal limitation—with only one branch location and business hours ending at 4pm weekdays, this institution is inaccessible to most consumers outside the immediate local area or those unable to visit during standard business hours.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
